properties.remark: This tornado snapped and uprooted trees at the Henryetta Golf and Country Club, and damaged several outbuildings. Homes were damaged and outbuildings were destroyed when it crossed Lake Road. This tornado was rated EF-1 with maximum wind of 90 to 100 mph based on the damage.
This tornado snapped several trees on New Lake Road, crossed I-40, and damaged several outbuildings on Holly Road. This tornado was rated EF-1 with maximum wind of 90 to 95 mph based on the damage.
This tornado rolled an RV, snapped large tree limbs, damaged the roof of a home, and snapped several power poles. This tornado was rated EF-1 with maximum wind of 90 to 95 mph based on the damage.
This tornado snapped large tree limbs and damaged the roof of a home. This tornado was rated EF-0 with maximum wind of 75 to 80 mph based on the damage.
Power lines down.
Metal barn destroyed. Time estimated from Radar.
A tornado damaged outbuildings and homes, and blew down trees.
This tornado snapped large tree limbs, damaged the roof of a home, and blew the roof off an outbuilding. This tornado was rated EF-0 with maximum wind of 80 to 85 mph based on the damage.
Power lines down.

A tornado damaged multiple outbuildings, snapped power poles, and snapped large tree limbs. Estimated max wind in the tornado based on this damage was 80 to 85 mph, thus it was rated EF-0.

A tornado developed over Grand Lake, moved across Patricia Island, and then moved across the north side of Grove. It uprooted or snapped trees, damaged homes, destroyed outbuildings, and snapped numerous large tree limbs. Based on this damage, it was rated EF1.

An EF1 tornado with estimated wind speeds of 90 to 100 mph was confirmed north of Jay. The tornado uprooted trees, damaged homes and destroyed outbuildings. Additional information will be provided once the survey is complete.
A tornado uprooted a few trees and snapped numerous large tree limbs. Based on this damage, it was rated EF1.

An EF1 tornado was confirmed with maximum winds in the range of 95 to 105 mph on the north side of Gravette. Uprooted and snapped trees and damage to homes were observed. Additional information will be provided once the survey team gets back to the office.

A tornado moved the area in and around the community of Liberty. Trees were uprooted, damage was done to the roof of a school, and a power pole was damaged.
A tornado briefly occurred near the Port of Van Buren. It formed on the southwest side of the Arkansas River and moved northeast into the port. Damage was done to two structures.
significant damage to a home... likely tornado damage.

A tornado occurred in southeast Van Buren and did the most damage just south of the Baptist Health-Van Buren complex. Several homes were damaged. One home completely lost its roof, and another lost a substantial part of its roof.

A tornado developed near Pyle Mountain and moved east-southeast into northwest McAlester... dissipating after crossing Main Street in north McAlester. While the tornado was weakening, damaging winds were increasing across McAlester.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from Mcalester. Extensive damage ito buildings in downtown McAlester.
